note: Round-2 /innovate: hunt a concretely novel MECHANISM (round 1 reframed the product). Thesis: the curated catalog is ALREADY an interventional edit->effect knowledge base. UNSTAMPED — engine stamps via --run-ledger.

candidates.claim: Make the curated catalog GENERATIVE: predict the phenotype effect of an UNSEEN edit by interpolating across a site's already-catalogued substitutions (any non-WT substitution at a multiply-catalogued resistance site tends to confer resistance) — this directly serves 'guess the phenotype of a minor modification', the original north-star framing, and turns a lookup table into a predictor.

Cross the tree-of-life census with the federation: a target-site catalog is PROTEIN-FAMILY-level, not organism-level, so a validated catalog TRANSFERS to a new organism with ZERO new labels — the fastest way to add census cells (bypasses the label wall for mechanism-conserved targets).

Abduce WHY the catalog beats learned models: not because it is curated, but because each entry is a background-INDEPENDENT interventional (edit->effect) statement that transfers across genetic backgrounds where an observational correlation cannot. Design consequence: BUILD the genome world model as a catalog-shaped interventional KB, and seed every new cell from interventional experiments (DMS / marker-transfer), never from observational isolate mining.

Relax 'a learned world model needs external phenotype labels' -> train a generative effect-predictor SELF-SUPERVISED on the catalog's own edit->effect entries: the pooled cross-cell catalog is thousands of interventional statements across organisms, which IS a training set for 'predict the effect of an edit' — no new wet-lab label required.
CONTROL (tempting-but-wrong): gene-wide interpolation — ANY mutation ANYWHERE in the gene predicts effect, so the generative catalog can extrapolate freely across the whole protein.
